text.title: inc-tool-tier-approval-gate Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for inc-tool-tier-approval-gate. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: The tool executor MUST classify every callable into T0/T1/T2; MUST allow T0 unconditionally with audit; MUST allow T1 unconditionally with audit; MUST refuse T2 unless a signed approval token is supplied; MUST treat any tool absent from the registry as T2; and MUST NOT honor tier overrides from the agent prompt or tool arguments.
rule.rationale: Centralizing the gate at the executor makes safety a property of the system rather than the prompt. Prompt-level "ask before deleting" instructions are routinely bypassed by jailbreaks, confusion, or follow-up tool-use loops; a registry lookup is mechanical.

rule.statement: An approval token MUST be a JWT signed by the approval service; MUST contain `sub` (approver), `tool`, `target`, `incident_id`, `iat`, `exp` (≤ 300 seconds after `iat`), and `jti`; MUST be single-use (server-side `jti` revocation on first acceptance); and MUST be rejected when any claim mismatches the actual call's `(tool, target, incident_id)` arguments.
rule.rationale: An unscoped or replayable token degrades to a "global yes". Binding to the exact `(tool, target, incident)` triple stops a captured token from being reused on a different cluster, and the 5-minute TTL stops it from being held for a future incident.
